goal: Enable safe production insertion for eligible normal scalar settings that currently use Hyprland defaults, without expanding unsafe write classes.
whatWasImplemented: Wired missing/default insertion into the existing safe-batch planner and executor for the narrow approved production scope.
Added insertion-specific plan records that show the target file, official setting, normalized config key, proposed value, exact inserted line, and review copy.

Restricted production insertion to reviewed single-file normal scalar configs with an existing normal root file that is not a symlink, generated, script-managed, duplicate, high-risk, display/render-risk, structured, profile/mode, runtime-only, or ambiguous.
Reused the safe-batch backup path, backup byte-equality proof, write path, reread verification, and restore-on-failure machinery for insertion changes.
Added deterministic safe-batch insertion section rendering and exact reread verification for inserted setting/value/line.
Updated live scenario harness coverage so the missing/default-only safe-env scenario proves the newly eligible single-root insertion path.
Kept unsafe missing/default layouts blocked, including managed, duplicate, multi-file/ambiguous, high-risk, structured, profile/mode, and runtime cases.
Added a source/include insertion target-selection readiness model that allows only selected single-root targets and keeps multi-target or managed layouts disabled.
Added deterministic tests proving source/include and managed target selection remain blocked while the current single-root path stays eligible.
Connected the source/include insertion readiness model to a disabled setting-detail review surface for missing/default rows in connected layouts.
The disabled review surface shows root config, candidate target files, selected target state, readiness state, and disabled planned target controls.
Added source-level UI tests proving the source/include target-selection review is visible, read-only, disabled, and does not invoke production write or runtime code.
Added source/include fixture target-selection proof with selected target preconditions for root path, selected target, source depth, management hints, and candidate count.
The fixture proof blocks no target selected, unknown target, generated/script-managed targets, symlink/profile targets, and duplicate/ambiguous layouts.
The fixture proof allows only temp-fixture planning readiness and never enables production source/include insertion.
Added source/include selected-target dry-run planner that consumes selected-target fixture proof and the existing missing/default insertion plan.
The dry-run planner shows exact selected target, source depth, inserted line, and preview copy without writing files.
The dry-run planner blocks no selection, managed/ambiguous selections, target mismatch, non-fixture targets, and insertion plans that are already blocked.
Wired the selected-target dry-run preview into the disabled detail UI so users can inspect root path, selected target, source depth, proposed value, planned inserted line, and dry-run status.

Added a guarded temp-fixture executor that consumes source/include target-selection proof and the dry-run plan, backs up the selected target, inserts the exact planned line, rereads it, restores original bytes, and verifies restored content fingerprint.

Added copied-config-tree source/include proof that maps original source/include files to temp copies, runs the guarded executor against copied targets, restores copied files, and verifies originals unchanged.

Added a default-disabled source/include production gate review that requires explicit selected target, target-in-graph proof, matching dry-run plan, copied-config-tree proof, and production flag before any connected-file production expansion.
whatWasNotImplemented: No insertion into generated, script-managed, symlink/current-profile, profile/mode, multi-file, duplicate, high-risk, display/render-risk, structured, runtime-only, or missing target files was enabled.
No real user config insertion was attempted.
No runtime reload or mutating hyprctl action was added.
No production duplicate, high-risk, structured, profile/mode, runtime, or Hyprland 0.55.4 migration behavior was enabled.

safetyBoundaries.allowedTarget: eligible normal scalar missing/default setting in an explicit existing single-file root config

blockers: Production insertion remains intentionally limited to single-file normal scalar targets.

Source/include insertion now has readiness model, disabled UI, fixture target-selection proof, selected-target dry-run planner, preview UI, temp-fixture guarded executor, copied-config-tree proof, and default-disabled production gate review, but production connected-file insertion remains disabled until explicit approval and live restoration proof exist.
Managed-file insertion, duplicate insertion, high-risk/display insertion, structured insertion, profile/mode insertion, and runtime mutation remain blocked.
nextRecommendedWork: Add explicit approval flow and live restoration proof before considering source/include production insertion beyond single-root safe-batch scope.
